pi_items.verified_value: Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra (synthetic - dynamically assigned)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 24-35: Product is not in seed data. Script queries for product by title, and if not found in seed, takes ANY existing product and renames it to 'Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra'. seed_products.php contains 12 different products (Sony Headphones, MacBook Air, Samsung TV, Bose Earbuds, Logitech Mouse, Dell Monitor, Anker Charger, Keychron Keyboard, WD Black SSD, Razer Mouse, CalDigit Dock, Corsair RAM) but NO 'Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra'.
seed_products.php line 26 and setup_drupal_commerce.sh line 229 both create store with name='Urban Electronics'
seed_products.php line 27 and setup_drupal_commerce.sh line 230 both create store with mail='store@urbanelectronics.com'
seed_promotions.php line 77 creates promotion with name='Electronics 15% Off'

This is the TARGET corrected date, not the seed value. seed_promotions.php does not set end_date for Electronics 15% Off. setup_task.sh line 61 intentionally sets end_date='2024-01-01' (past date) as the broken state. The task requires fixing it to '2025-12-31'. verifier.py line 82 validates that end_date contains '2025-12-31'.
setup_drupal_commerce.sh line 249 creates user 'mikewilson' with email 'mike.wilson@example.com' and password 'Customer123!'

privileged_info_summary: This task operates on a Drupal Commerce environment with synthetically generated data. Verified facts: The store 'Urban Electronics' is created in seed scripts with correct email 'store@urbanelectronics.com'. The promotion 'Electronics 15% Off' exists in seed data. The user 'mikewilson' is created with email 'mike.wilson@example.com'. The target product 'Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra' is NOT in the 12-product seed dataset; instead, setup_task.sh dynamically renames whichever product exists to this name if the exact product is missing, making it synthetically assigned. The correct end date for the promotion is '2025-12-31' (set as the target fix, not the initial seed state which has no end_date).
